content:
You open YouTube and search for AI interview tips.
One video explains prompt engineering.
Another video covers transformer math.
A blog post lists 100 machine learning questions.
A creator shares the latest AI framework.
After a week of studying, you feel busy.
You do not feel prepared.
Many AI engineers face this problem.
They spend months collecting information.
They rarely build a clear preparation plan.
Then the interview starts.
The interviewer asks a question that sounds nothing like the content they studied.
“Design a RAG system that handles 100,000 documents with low latency.”
“How would you detect hallucinations in production?”
“Your AI agent entered an infinite loop. How would you debug it?”
The problem is not a lack of effort.
